regulatory_snapshot: Post-shortage: tirzepatide (resolved Dec 2024) & semaglutide (resolved Feb 2025). 503B cannot compound GLP-1s from bulk. 503A limited to 3 legal pathways: documented allergy, non-commercial dose/route, combination formulation with significant difference. PCAC meeting July 23-24, 2026 to discuss BPC-157, TB-500, MOTs-C, KPV, Semax, Epitalon, DSIP for 503A Bulks List. FDA issued 30+ warning letters to telehealth companies (Mar 2026). FDA 503B list: 96 registered facilities (up from 93). 14 peptides moved to FDA Category 1 status.

pharmacies.name: MediVera Compounding Pharmacy
pharmacies.pcab_source: Dual PCAB accreditation (sterile & non-sterile) claimed on website
red_flags: No direct email — contact form only
Peptide services implied under 'Longevity' — not explicitly listed
pharmacies.brief: MediVera Compounding Pharmacy (Score: 14/15) — Dual PCAB-accredited (sterile & non-sterile) | 48-state licensed | 25+ years | 56,000 sq ft facility (March 2025) | $60K+/month third-party testing | ISO-7 cleanrooms, ISO-5 hoods | USP 795/797/800 compliant | 120+ person team | 7 therapeutic categories including weight management (GLP-1 support compounds) and longevity. Explicitly targets telehealth partnerships. Contact: 877-531-1147, contact form at mediverarx.com. Angle: <1% of pharmacies have dual PCAB — premium credentialing. Built for telehealth partnerships. High-volume infrastructure.
